Defines the RangeError.prototype built-in object and its properties for the JerryScript engine.

# Purpose
This code defines the prototype object for the `RangeError` built-in object in a JavaScript engine. It is part of the implementation of the ECMAScript standard, specifically focusing on the `RangeError` object, which is used to indicate an error when a value is not within the set or range of allowed values. The code uses macros to define the properties and methods associated with the `RangeError.prototype` object.

The `OBJECT_ID` macro assigns an identifier to the `RangeError.prototype` object, while the `OBJECT_VALUE` and `STRING_VALUE` macros define the properties of the prototype. The `OBJECT_VALUE` macro is used to set the `constructor` property, linking it to the `RangeError` object. The `STRING_VALUE` macro defines the `name` and `message` properties, with the `name` property set to "RangeError" and the `message` property initialized as an empty string. These properties are marked as configurable and writable, allowing them to be modified or deleted in accordance with the ECMAScript specification.
